concepts.title: File Modes
concepts.description: There are two types of file modes: binary and text mode. Binary mode is used to read and write binary files, while text mode is used to read and write text files.

concepts.title: File Operations
concepts.description: The following are the basic file operations: open(), close(), read(), write(), seek(), and tell().

concepts.title: File Object
concepts.description: The file object is used to perform file operations. It is created using the open() function and must be closed after use to avoid memory leaks.

concepts.title: File Modes Mnemonic
concepts.description: To remember the binary and text modes, use the mnemonic: Binary - BYTES, Text - TEXT. B and T stand for the first letter of each mode, and BYTES and TEXT are easy to remember.

concepts.title: File Not Found Error
concepts.description: Students often forget to check if a file exists before trying to open it. To avoid this, use the exists() function to check if a file exists before attempting to open it.

concepts.title: File Permissions
concepts.description: File permissions are a set of rights that determine what actions can be performed on a file. The three basic permissions are read (r), write (w), and execute (x).

concepts.title: File Buffering
concepts.description: File buffering is a technique used to improve file performance by buffering data in memory before writing it to the file. It can be disabled using the buffering parameter in the open() function.

concepts.title: Exception Handling
concepts.description: Exception handling is crucial when working with files. Use try-except blocks to catch and handle exceptions that may occur during file operations, such as file not found or permission errors.
